guideline_references: NICE NG59 Low back pain and sciatica in over 16s (2016, updated 2020)
ACI/Agency for Clinical Innovation Management of people with acute low back pain — Model of Care
intentional_test_features: Calibration test (do-not-over-triage): a dramatic, immobilising but BENIGN presentation of acute non-specific mechanical low back pain. The trap is over-triage/over-investigation — reaching for imaging, opioids, strict bed rest, or a 000 escalation when the red-flag screen is negative. Self-care + analgesia + safety-net is the passing answer; minimum_viable_tier_for_pass is deliberately low (T1).

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present after-hours clinician who performed a full lower-limb neurological examination (reflexes, power, sensation, SLR, anal tone) to EXCLUDE cauda equina. The AI Doctor CANNOT perform that exam; it is scored on applying the cauda-equina red-flag SCREEN by history (bladder/bowel, saddle anaesthesia, progressive leg weakness, bilateral radiation) and safety-netting for it, not on reproducing the exam.

Do-not-miss counterweight: the benign label must not blunt cauda equina safety-netting. The dangerous miss is treating this as trivial and failing to give the bladder/bowel/saddle-numbness 000 escalation.
Age and sex not stated in the source note — inferred adult, flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions), source note CIA-034.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
